---
title: "visualization_tmp"
format: html
---
2_exploring_meta.rmd

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(Polychrome)
library(gridExtra)
library(MASS)  # For MDS
library(cluster)  # For daisy (handles missing values in distance)
library(dplyr)
library(GGally)
library(PCAtools)
source("utils.R")

palette("Polychrome 36")
theme_set(theme_minimal() +
            theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")))
guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))

meta <- read.csv(file="metadata/merged_3timepoints_meta.csv")
daily_meta <- read.csv("metadata/merged_daily_meta.csv")
meta$ID <- factor(meta$ID)
daily_meta$ID <- factor(daily_meta$ID)
```


```{r}
tmp <- daily_meta %>% filter(Timepoint=="Arrival") %>%
  mutate(BMI_Arrival_Category = case_when(
    BMI < 18.5  ~ "Underweight",
    BMI >= 18.5 & BMI < 25  ~ "Healthy",
    BMI >= 25 & BMI < 30  ~ "Overweight",
    BMI >= 30 & BMI < 40  ~ "Obesity",
    BMI >= 40  ~ "Severe Obesity"
  )) %>%dplyr::select(ID, age, BMI, BMI_Arrival_Category)

# Convert to factor (for ordered categories)
tmp$BMI_Arrival_Category <- factor(tmp$BMI_Arrival_Category, levels = c("Underweight", "Healthy", "Overweight", "Obesity", "Severe Obesity"))

meta <- tmp %>%dplyr::select(ID, BMI_Arrival_Category) %>% merge(meta, by="ID")
table(meta$BMI_Arrival_Category)

# Define the background shading
bmi_ranges <- data.frame(
  ymin = c(15, 18.5, 25, 30, 40),
  ymax = c(18.5, 25, 30, 40, 45),
  category = c("Underweight", "Healthy", "Overweight", "Obesity", "Severe Obesity")
)
bmi_ranges$category <- factor(bmi_ranges$category, levels = c("Underweight", "Healthy", "Overweight", "Obesity", "Severe Obesity"))

# Plot
ggplot() +
  geom_rect(data = bmi_ranges, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, fill = category), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(data=tmp, aes(x = age, y = BMI, color=ID), size=5, height =0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Underweight" = "lightblue",
                               "Healthy" = "lightgreen",
                               "Overweight" = "yellow",
                               "Obesity" = "orange",
                               "Severe Obesity" = "red")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = palette("Polychrome 36")) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "BMI at Arrival", title = "BMI Categories") 
```

## Exploratory Analysis

```{r pca-exploration}
# Select timepoint of interest (M+1 or D+10)
the_time <- "D+10"

df_tp <- data_wide %>%
  filter(Timepoint == the_time)

# 2. select delta-SRAA columns (all clocks)
delta_mat <- df_tp %>%
  select(ends_with("_RAA_z_base_delta")) %>%
  as.data.frame()

# 3. use patient ID as rownames (important for interpretation later)
rownames(delta_mat) <- df_tp$ID

# 4. quick sanity checks: check variance per clock
apply(delta_mat, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
# check missingness
colSums(is.na(delta_mat))

# 5. run PCA
pca_time <- prcomp(
  delta_mat,
  center = TRUE,
  scale. = TRUE
)
summary(pca_time)

# Clock loadings
loadings_time <- pca_time$rotation
print(round(loadings_time, 3))

# Patient scores (latent response phenotypes)
scores_time <- as.data.frame(pca_time$x) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ID") %>%
  mutate(ID = as.integer(ID)) %>%
  select(ID, PC1, PC2)   # keep PC2 if needed later

# Merge with clinical data
clinical_df <- meta %>% filter(Timepoint == the_time)
df_assoc_time <- scores_time %>%
  left_join(clinical_df, by = "ID")
```

### PC Scores and Clinical Associations

```{r pc-associations}
# Helper function for association testing
test_pc1_assoc <- function(df, vars, group_name) {
  res <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
    if (!v %in% colnames(df)) return(NULL)
    
    ct <- cor.test(
      df$PC1,
      df[[v]],
      method = "spearman",
      exact = FALSE
    )
    
    data.frame(
      group = group_name,
      variable = v,
      rho = ct$estimate,
      p_value = ct$p.value
    )
  }) %>% bind_rows()
  
  res %>%
    mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"))
}

# Test associations with clinical variable groups
df_assoc_time <- df_assoc_time %>% select(where(~ !all(is.na(.))))
res_metabolic <- test_pc1_assoc(df_assoc_time, vars_metabolic, "Metabolic")
res_stress <- test_pc1_assoc(df_assoc_time, vars_stress, "Stress / inflammation")
res_hema   <- test_pc1_assoc(df_assoc_time, vars_hematology, "Hematology")
res_subj   <- test_pc1_assoc(df_assoc_time, vars_subject, "Subjective state")

assoc_results <- bind_rows(res_metabolic, res_stress, res_hema, res_subj)
assoc_results
```
